Job Title: Senior Director - Workforce Planning, Reporting and Systems
Ad Text:
Employment Type:Full Time, Permanent
Classification:Senior Officer Grade A (P46906)
Salary$172,246 (Plus 12% Super)
Location: The Canberra Hospital, Garran
Section: People and Culture
Closing Date: 1 April 2026





What can we offer you:



Competitive pay rates and excellent working conditions within a tertiary hospital.

Salary Packaging with many options that provide full fringe benefits tax concessions.

Flexible working conditions.

Access to Employee Assistance Program.

Access to onsite Physiotherapists.

Access to onsite cafes, staff cafeteria, pharmacy and gift shop.

About the Role:


Canberra Health Services is seeking a dynamic and future focused Senior Director to lead an expanding workforce planning, reporting and systems function at a time of significant opportunity and organisational focus. This role offers a rare chance to shape how workforce planning, data and analytics are understood, valued and embedded across CHS leadership, directly influencing how the organisation plans for and delivers sustainable healthcare.





Reporting to the Executive Branch Manager, People and Culture, the Senior Director partners closely with executives and senior leaders to lift workforce capability, strengthen workforce literacy and further mature data informed decision making across CHS. The role provides strategic leadership over workforce analytics and reporting, ensuring workforce data integrity supports operational decisions, statutory reporting and timely responses to executive and government requirements, including Questions on Notice.





Leading a specialist multidisciplinary team, this position is central to positioning People and Culture as a trusted, value adding partner to CHS operations. The role balances enterprise wide consistency with flexible, locally responsive workforce solutions, enabling leaders to confidently navigate complexity and deliver frontline outcomes.





This is an exciting senior leadership opportunity for a confident, influential workforce professional who is passionate about using data to drive insight, building leadership capability and making a lasting impact on the future workforce of the ACT's public health system.


For more information regarding the position duties click here for the .

Please note prior to commencement successful candidates will be required to:



Undergo a pre-employment National Police Check.

Comply with Canberra Health Services Occupational Assessment, Screening and Vaccination policy, as a Category B role.


To Apply:

Please apply online by submitting a copy of your CV along with a 2-page cover letter or pitch, responding to the Selection Criteria, listed in the Position Description.





***Please note applications submitted via an agency will not be accepted for this position***




Canberra Health Services (CHS) is focussed on the delivery of high quality, effective, person centred care. It provides acute, sub-acute, primary and community‐based health services, to the Australian Capital Territory (ACT) and surrounding region. More information can be found on the .



Our Vision: creating exceptional health care together
Our Role: to be a health service that is trusted by our community
Our Values: Reliable, Progressive, Respectful and Kind



CHS is committed to workforce diversity and to creating an inclusive workplace. As part of this commitment, we welcome applications from all diversity groups. Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability and people who identify as LGBTQIA+ are particularly encouraged to apply.

Ad Text:
Do work that makes a difference!


Contribute to nationally significant projects supporting Australia's real‑time payments systems

Develop your career in a collaborative, high‑performing environment

Access extensive staff benefits, including on‑site gym facilities, fitness classes and canteen


About the Role/Team

The Payments Settlements (PS) department is seeking a data-oriented Lead Business Analyst to uplift, modernise and maintain the data warehouse supporting critical payments systems-including the Reserve Bank Information and Transfer System (RITS), the Fast Settlement Service (FSS), and SWIFT interbank payment systems.

You will be a key member of the Business Delivery section and will work as an important bridge between business and technology, ensuring requirements are clearly defined and aligned for the successful delivery of the projects that support/ enhance the Australian payments system. This role will initially sit with the FSS Next Program, a major 3-5‑year transformation initiative that will enhance real‑time, 24/7 settlement capabilities for the New Payments Platform (NPP), which processes an average of 3.5 million payments per day, worth $4 billion. In this program, you will evaluate various data platform options, contribute to business case development, and help deliver a new technology solution capable of managing large‑scale transactional datasets.

What you will do

Engage with business owners, SMEs and technical teams to elicit, interpret and document business requirements

Identify and map data assets & data products, end-to-end processes, data lineage, ownership and map business workflows to data flows.

Facilitate structured workshops for discovery, ideation and requirements gathering for the new data platform.

Collaborate with data analysts, architects, developers and design teams to convert business needs into functional specifications for scalable data warehouse and technology solutions.

Support data modelling and data preparation for downstream analytics

Create and maintain comprehensive documentation, including user stories for Agile delivery.

Prepare and present high-quality decision support materials, including options and trade-off analysis, to senior leadership and executive stakeholders

Identify risks, interdependencies, and cross-functional impacts across business and technology domains

Manage requirement changes using structured change‑control processes and maintain traceability.

Contribute to change‑management planning and execution.

Contribute to the enhancement of business analysis practice, tools, and templates, and mentor junior analysts.


Your background

To be successful in this critical role you will possess:

Advanced knowledge of business analysis methods, elicitation techniques and documentation standards

Strong proficiency with JIRA, Confluence, Azure DevOps or similar tools

Experience in data platforms, data‑transformation or modernisation programs

Solid understanding of data assets, data products, data lineage and transformation techniques

Experience with structured and semi‑structured datasets and data engineering delivery practices

Strong process mapping and workflow design skills

Deep knowledge of data warehouse architecture, ETL processes and data modelling

A "technical BA" mindset, comfortable working with database schemas and ERDs

Strong SQL skills for data analysis, data mapping, extraction and profiling

Excellent analytical, multi-tasking, organizational and stakeholder management skills.

Demonstrated experience as a Business Analyst in data-centric environments or projects, with a strong focus on data warehousing, analytics, and/or enterprise transformation initiatives

A professional certification in business analysis such as IIBA CBAP would be highly regarded.



Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

We’re seeking a Data Integration Engineer to join a large, customer-focused organisation operating within a complex enterprise environment. This is a greenfield-style role supporting a major integration program, where you’ll play a key part in connecting core business systems and enabling reliable, scalable data across the organisation.

In this role, you’ll design and build data pipelines that integrate critical systems across finance, HR, payroll, and external platforms. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to ensure data is accurate, accessible, and aligned to business needs, while driving improvements across data quality, performance, and governance.

Your day-to-day will include:

Integrating data from financial systems, banking platforms, and taxation/reporting systems
Developing and managing data flows from recruitment and external data sources
Collaborating with stakeholders to translate data requirements into scalable solutions
Ensuring data quality, integrity, and compliance across all systems
Monitoring, optimising, and improving data pipelines for performance and reliability
Documenting data architecture, mappings, and technical designs
About You:

Strong experience in data engineering, ETL pipelines, and data modelling
Experience working with ERP, HR, payroll, or finance datasets in enterprise environments
Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python, Scala, or similar
Experience with modern data platforms such as Databricks or Snowflake
Exposure to AWS data services or similar cloud tooling
Understanding of financial, payroll, or compliance data structures (highly regarded)
A structured approach to building reliable, scalable data solutions

Ad Text:
A career at ASIC will broaden and deepen your already accomplished knowledge, skills and abilities. By working on cutting edge issues, you'll make a difference to our goals, Australia's economic reputation and wellbeing and your own career aspirations 

ASIC's Data, Analytics and AI (AiDA) team is seeking a Senior Data Scientist to join their team. The successful candidate will provide comprehensive leadership in relation to data, insights, machine learning, and AI systems whin ASIC.

 

Sydney Location
24 Month Fixed Term Position
Salary from $152,074 to $181,284 (depending on experience) + 15.4 % Superannuation
Applications close at 11:59pm AEDT, Thursday 26 March 2026

 

The team 

The Data, Analytics and AI (AiDA) team is essential for ASIC's transformation into a digitally enabled, data-informed regulator. The data science team within AiDA collaborates with business units to develop machine learning solutions, utilizing the latest technologies to address regulatory and compliance challenges in surveillance and enforcement. 

 

The role

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be central to data, insights, machine learning, and AI. You will provide best practices, lead, and deliver high-impact machine learning and AI systems or products with stakeholders. Additionally, you will conduct experiments and assess evolving trends, emerging risks, and systemic misconduct in financial services and markets.

More specifically, you will: 

Present insights and learnings to key stakeholders and executives, driving accountability for action and impact
Provide thought leadership, upskill ASIC's data science practitioners and enhance capabilities in key techniques e.g. prompt engineering, RAG, fine-tuning and establish best practices for monitoring, testing and validating GenAI solutions
Conduct hands-on data analysis using exploratory analysis, advanced statistical methods, modelling, data visualization and storytelling techniques
Lead AI/ML solutions or products from initial brief to implementation, managing stakeholder expectations for timely delivery and usage
Design and improve operational processes for data collection, AI/ML model deployment and risk identification
Coordinate and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams to design and develop standardised, transferrable and reusable frameworks and architectures for AI/ ML solutions across ASIC

 

About you

The ideal candidate should have a strong background in data, insights, machine learning, and AI. They must excel in delivering high-impact AI/ML systems and providing best practices. Proficiency in data analysis, statistical methods, modelling, data visualization and storytelling is essential. Additionally, they should be skilled in designing and improving processes for data collection, AI/ML model deployment, and risk identification. Establishing and promoting MLOps capabilities for consistent ML workloads across ASIC is crucial.

 

In addition, you will have:

Tertiary qualifications in a quantitative discipline such as Applied Finance, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or Economics
5+ years of industry experience in AI, Machine Learning Engineering, Data Science
Demonstrated advanced level of proficiency in programming languages such as Python, R, SQL
Previous experience using data science machine learning platforms like Amazon SageMaker, Azure ML, Databricks, and Vertex AI
Experience in manipulating and analysing complex, semi-structured, or unstructured data to solve challenging problems
Experience in areas such as Generative AI, RAG Architecture, PySpark or Graph Analytics is advantageous
Strong communication skills with the ability to present insights to stakeholders and provide thought leadership at all levels
Proficiency in risk management strategies to identify, mitigate, and manage data or privacy related risks

 

Click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications close at AEDT, Thursday 26 March 2026.

 

About ASIC

ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms. 

ASIC is an equal opportunity employer seeking people who want to make a difference. ASIC is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Applications from people of all ages, cultural backgrounds, abilities, LGBTIQ+ identity, and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent are encouraged to apply.

 

We offer a range of employee benefits including:

Attractive superannuation
Additional leave entitlements
50/50 hybrid work-from home model
Flexible work arrangements
Assistance for study and professional development

Our Mission
At iMedX, our mission is to provide solutions that give time and money back to care.

What we do
iMedX are industry leaders in clinical documentation and revenue cycle management solutions, and now our solutions leverage AI technology.  Driven by our commitment to innovation and quality, iMedX delivers trusted AI capabilities and expertise to the healthcare industry, providing healthcare providers with state-of-the-art tools to streamline their workflows.

iMedX Difference

At iMedX, our solutions are built on years of experience, deep expertise, and a thorough understanding of the healthcare landscape in Australia and New Zealand. Our services use Generative AI technology, acting as a companion to healthcare professionals to allow more time for patient care or for complex tasks that require human expertise.

Our values are built on Trust, Innovative Technology, Continuous Improvement, Integrity, Quality, a Customer-Focused, People-Centered approach, and delivering Value in everything we do.

About the Opportunity

We are on the lookout for an experienced Senior AI Project Manager – Healthcare Technology (HCS Implementation Lead) to lead the delivery of our HIM Companion Suite (HCS) across healthcare organisations in Australia, the US, and the Middle East. 

This is a senior, hands-on delivery leadership role responsible for the end-to-end implementation of production grade healthcare AI solutions. You will lead complex programs from discovery through deployment and adoption, ensuring solutions are clinically aligned, operationally embedded, compliant, and trusted by users. 

You’ll work closely with HIM leaders, clinical coders, AI engineers, product teams, and healthcare executives, translating advanced AI capability into real world outcomes that improve quality, confidence, and performance across revenue cycle operations. 

What You’ll Be Doing 

Leading end to end implementation of iMedX’s HIM Companion Suite (HCS) 

Owning delivery across AI enabled coding, workflow automation, QA, analytics, and optimisation 

Coordinating globally distributed teams across multiple time zones 

Managing governance, risk, compliance, and executive level reporting 

Driving stakeholder engagement, change management, and adoption 

Ensuring solutions are production ready, safe, explainable, and fit for purpose in regulated healthcare environments 

What You’ll Bring to iMedX

To thrive in this role, you’ll have:

Proven experience delivering AI solutions into production 

Strong healthcare technology delivery experience (provider side preferred) 

Working knowledge of Health Information Management (HIM) and clinical coding workflows 

Experience with healthcare interoperability (HL7, FHIR, APIs)      

7+ years leading complex technology or digital transformation programs 

Strong executive presence and stakeholder management capability 

Why Join iMedX?

At iMedX, we’re deeply committed to investing in our people. Our supportive, collaborative culture - extended to our growing virtual workforce - has led to an impressive eNPS score of 44 (January 2026). 

We’re proud of the positive, values-driven environment we’ve built at iMedX, where making decisions guided by our core values  is part of our everyday work.

As part of our team, you’ll benefit from:

Partner with some of the largest healthcare providers across ANZ, the US, and the Middle East 

Work on real AI in production, not pilots or proof of concept  

Combine deep healthcare expertise with advanced AI capability 

Be part of a growing organisation shaping the future of HIM and revenue cycle management

Employee Referral Program – Earn up to $2,000 for referring great talent

Access to iMedX’s AI Companion tool - cutting-edge Gen AI, this tool acts as an expert HIM in chat format to answer any question based on Australian Coding Standards and practices to help improve clinical documentation and revenue outcomes.

Flexibility – work remotely from home 

Work from Anywhere - up to 2 weeks per year you can "work from anywhere" within your usual domestic time zone.

Exclusive learning opportunities, Participate in bi-annual internal coder conferences and our iMedX annual Conference

Annual Conference and End of Year Party – We bring the entire team to Melbourne for an all-expenses-paid iMedX Conference, combining professional development with our year-end celebration. It’s our way of upskilling our team and showing that we genuinely value the time we spend together.

Opportunity to Have Your Say – We conduct bi-annual engagement surveys to track our eNPS and genuinely listen to our team’s feedback. We’re committed to taking action based on what you share

Career Growth – explore career pathways from Coding to Auditing and the opportunity to work across a diverse range of public and private health services in Australia and New Zealand

iMedX Study Support Program – Personalized support tailored to your individual career goals

Travel opportunities engage with clients onside when needed

Wellness Leave

Birthday leave

Monthly (all in) Town Hall Meetings – to help you stay connected and informed

New Starter Welcome Pack – Kick off your iMedX journey with branded merch to make choosing your work attire effortless and fun

A supportive, inclusive culture that prioritizes collaboration 

Monthly ‘in house’ professional development sessions – coding questions answered in “real time” by experienced Coders, plus extensive library of education topics and more!

 Ready to Make a Difference with iMedX?” 
Join us in revolutionizing healthcare documentation and coding. Apply now to become part of a supportive, innovative team that values your expertise. Don’t miss this opportunity—apply today!”

You'll gain hands-on exposure to a live D365 FinOps environment supporting a global travel company — with real project ownership from day one. Job Title: Senior Financial Analyst - Canberra or Sydney - Hybrid
Ad Text:

Position Description: Energy Market Analyst

Location: Brisbane, Queensland

Reports to: Director, Networks

Company Overview:

Blackstart is a dynamic and growing professional services firm recognised for its expertise in navigating the technical, commercial, and regulatory complexities of the energy transition. Our work spans the Australian and South-East Asian energy markets, supporting high impact projects across generation, transmission, storage, and large energy users. We pride ourselves on a collaborative environment, meaningful professional development opportunities, and a commitment to innovation.

Role Overview:

As an Energy Market Analyst, you will play a central role in modelling, analysing, and interpreting energy market dynamics to support client projects and strategic decision making. You will develop and run electricity market models, assess project economics, analyse market scenarios, and contribute to commercial and regulatory insights.

Key Responsibilities:

Energy Market Modelling & Analysis:

- Build, calibrate, and run energy market models to assess dispatch outcomes, price forecasts, and market behaviour.

- Develop long term market scenarios including sensitivities.

- Analyse results to support project feasibility, investment cases, network connection strategies, and commercial assessments.

- Interpret model outputs into insights suitable for technical and executive audiences.

Commercial & Financial Insight:

- Support project valuation and commercial modelling across renewable, storage, hydrogen, and hybrid assets.

- Assess revenue streams such as arbitrage, FCAS, capacity products, PPAs, and emerging markets.

- Identify market risks and opportunities across investment horizons.

Regulatory & Policy Analysis:

- Monitor regulatory developments across the NEM and WEM

- Assess impacts of policy and rule changes on client strategy and market outcomes.

- Contribute to submissions and strategy papers.

Reporting & Communication:

- Prepare high‑quality reports, presentations, dashboards, and visualisations.

- Present findings to clients and internal stakeholders.

- Communicate complex modelling concepts in clear, plain language.

Client Engagement & Collaboration:

- Work with clients to understand project needs and modelling assumptions.

- Collaborate with technical specialists, and commercial analysts.

- Support proposal development and study delivery.

Innovation & Continuous Improvement:

- Recommend improvements to modelling workflows and datasets.

- Stay up to date with emerging technologies and market frameworks.

- Support internal capability building.

Qualifications & Experience:

Education:

- Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Economics, Mathematics, Data Science, or related field; a Master's degree is highly desirable.

Technical & Analytical Skills:

- Strong quantitative skills with experience in market or power system modelling.

- Experience with PLEXOS, PSO, Aurora, BID3, or similar tools. Experience with PLEXOS is highly advantageous.

- Proficiency in Python, R, VBA, and SQL desirable.

Industry Experience:

- 2–6 years in electricity markets, energy economics, market modelling, or similar fields.

- Prior energy sector experience is a requirement for the role.

- Familiarity with NEM structure and regulatory frameworks.

- Working knowledge of Australian energy regulatory requirements is required for the role.

Communication & Collaboration:

- Excellent written and verbal communication.

Company description:



About VIDA

About VIDA The Victorian Infrastructure Delivery Authority (VIDA) is a specialist project delivery organisation, responsible for delivering the biggest transport and health infrastructure program in Victorian history. This includes more than 200 projects such as the Metro Tunnel, North East Link, West Gate Tunnel, removing level crossings, new and upgraded hospitals in Footscray, Frankston and Warrnambool, and specialist programs.



Job description:



About VIDA Health

VIDA Health (Victorian Health Building Authority) is responsible for the rollout of the Victorian Government's multi-billion-dollar health capital projects. We're delivering world-class health facilities across Victoria including new public hospitals and emergency care, community health services, ambulance branches, residential aged care and mental health facilities. VIDA Health also provides technical support across engineering, property, asset management and environmental sustainability disciplines.

The Opportunity

As Manager, Project Data Governance, you will lead a team of analysts within the Project Controls branch to ensure project data is accurate, consistent, well-governed and fit for purpose. Your work will underpin critical internal and external reporting to senior executives, Ministers, central agencies and the public.

This is a senior leadership role focused on establishing strong data governance frameworks, driving quality and assurance across project reporting, and enabling meaningful insights through modern analytics and visualisation tools.

Working closely with project teams, Reporting & Analytics, and key stakeholders across VIDA and government, you will ensure project data supports informed decision-making across the full project lifecycle.


Key aspects of the role include:

Establishing and maintaining data governance standards, definitions and quality frameworks for project data.
Leading a team of analysts to deliver high-quality insights and dashboards using tools such as Power BI.
Overseeing the coordination, quality assurance and delivery of monthly project and program reporting.
Supporting external reporting, ministerial briefs and audit requirements with defensible, accurate data.
Driving innovation and continuous improvement across project data, reporting and governance practices.
Ensuring compliance with legislative, privacy and information security requirements.

We are looking for:

A data governance or reporting leader with experience in complex, enterprise-scale environments.
Strong expertise in data quality, governance frameworks and assurance processes
Experience working with analytics and reporting platforms such as Power BI, Excel/Power Query, SharePoint and SQL-based environments.
Proven ability to translate complex data into clear insights for executive and non-technical audiences.
A confident leader who builds capability, influences stakeholders and drives continuous improvement.

To learn more about the opportunity and view the full requirements please download a copy of the Position Description HERE.

Qualifications
Tertiary qualification in data governance, business, analytics, risk, project management or a related discipline, or demonstrated equivalent professional experience.
Experience within project controls, PMO or large infrastructure delivery environments will be highly regarded.

AEMO at the Heart of Energy

 AEMO is Australia’s independent energy system and market operator and system planner. AEMO’s purpose is to ensure safe, reliable and affordable energy today, and enable the energy transition for the benefit of all Australians.  Australian governments are now committed to a net zero economy for Australia by 2050 and our vision is to enable this.

We have the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to co-design the future of our energy systems, and our core values revolve around character, commitment, and connection.

 Join us as we contribute to this significant transition in the energy sector!

 About the Team & the Position

 The System Design Division plays a critical role in AEMO’s success, driving the organisation’s collaborative and industry-wide efforts in meeting the challenges of a transitioning energy system. The Division includes specialist areas in system planning, forecasting, future energy systems as well as onboarding and connections.

 The Market Operability team is seeking a Principal Analyst to undertake advanced energy modelling and analysis for the east coast gas and electricity systems. The team is responsible for the delivery of Gas Statement of Opportunities (GSOO), operability forecasting for the Integrated System Plan (ISP) for the National Electricity Market (NEM) and integrated gas and electricity modelling for the ISP.

 As a Principal Analyst, you will be responsible for providing strategic direction to key reports, overseeing modelling and analysis, and communicating results to various internal and external stakeholders. You will use a mix of forecast methods for the gas and electricity markets and modelling techniques focused on system optimisation over the short, medium and long-term. The Principal Analyst will lead model developments and improvements affecting short-term modelling to better analyse the reliability of the future energy system and support the development of the ISP

 Position Responsibilities:

Provide expert technical advice, expertise, and leadership on market modelling matters, including coordination of the GSOO process, leading short-term market modelling components of the ISP.

Apply advanced modelling methods, economic theory, and/or technical engineering knowledge to finalise model inputs and assumptions, run models, analyse results, and present analysis in written reports and verbal presentations.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

Support the Manager in people-management related processes, such as recruitment and development of staff, training, and regular feedback and performance appraisals.

Support the Manager in stakeholder coordination functions and facilitate industry forums to ensure that AEMO’s planning activities are effectively coordinated and communicated with stakeholders

 Position Requirements

Skills & Experience:

Proven experience in energy market simulation software (preferably PLEXOS) and forecasting tools and processes.

Demonstrated experience in energy market analysis (electricity and/or gas) and the interpretation of analysis performed by others.

Demonstrable experience in managing projects and people to achieve timely delivery of specified outcomes.

Knowledge and experience in analysing the physical operation of the electricity and/or gas systems and the overarching markets, and

understanding of power system reliability assessments.

Experience in writing technical reports and other documents that convey, in simple terms, complex ideas and analysis.

Very strong communication skills, particularly the ability to present modelling and analysis, and make recommendations to internal and external stakeholders

Education/ professional Certifications: 

Tertiary and/or post graduate qualifications in mathematics, economics, data science, physics or engineering or an appropriate technology discipline.

 In return some of our benefits to you

Flexible working: work from home, part time, job share, hybrid options, and additional leave options

Professional development via projects, industry networks, job rotation, study assistance and more.

Give back with up to 4 days of volunteering leave per year.

Reward your hard work with annual performance bonuses (subject to eligibility).

Embrace a healthier you with our wellness program, discounted health insurance, gym perks and our comprehensive Employee Assistance Program (EAP). 

 About Our Process 

At AEMO we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We welcome people of all backgrounds, including people with disabilities and neurodivergence and those who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander.

If you have any accessibility requirements to support you during the application or interview process, simply let us know by emailing us at talent@aemo.com.au

Ad Text:

About JANA

We are here for one reason: People. Together with our clients, we can change the lives of millions of Australians for the better.
JANA is a leading investment consulting firm with offices in Melbourne & Sydney. We provide investment advisory consulting to over 85 institutional clients, including superannuation funds, charities, insurers, foundations, and endowment funds. Our services include asset allocation, manager research and selection, and investment strategy advice. We foster close relationships with each client to understand their needs and identify unique investment opportunities for their benefit.

Your new role

The focus of this role is to work closely with the Quant and Risk Function to support the wider advisory business. This opportunity is best suited to an analytically oriented and motivated candidate with strong technical skills to help develop and support the quantitative tools and models that JANA uses to undertake investment research, formulate investment advice, and provide analysis and reporting. 

This role can be based in either our Sydney or Melbourne office and reports to the Head of Quant and Risk.

Some of your responsibilities will include:

Develop, maintain, and enhance quantitative models and tools to support investment research, portfolio construction, and risk analysis
Analyse portfolio performance, attribution, and risk across multi‑asset portfolios, including equities, fixed income, cash, and derivatives.
Build and manage data pipelines and database structures using internal and external data sources (e.g. market and vendor data)
Apply statistical techniques to large and complex datasets to generate insights, reports and dashboards for internal and external stakeholders
Create and maintain programmatic models to deliver systematic and repeatable analysis
Support the development of analytics and frameworks for performance attribution, risk monitoring and portfolio evaluation
Review and improve existing Excel and code-based models across the business
Contribute to the development of capital market assumptions and asset allocation analytics
About you 

Equally challenging and rewarding, this opportunity is perfect for someone with:

Strong quantitative, analytical, and problem‑solving skills, with a high level of attention to detail
Advanced capability in Excel, Python (or similar), and SQL, with an interest in using modern tools (including AI‑assisted workflows) to improve efficiency
Experience with data visualisation or business intelligence tools (e.g. Power BI, Dash) is desirable
Comfortable working with large, complex datasets and translating analysis into clear insights
A collaborative team player who enjoys project‑based work and working with diverse stakeholders
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non‑technical audiences
A proactive, self‑motivated learner who enjoys developing new skills and improving existing systems and processes
Tertiary qualifications in a relevant discipline preferred (flexibility for the right candidate); exposure to financial markets or superannuation is advantageous but not essential

At Lite n' Easy our goal is to create a healthier Australia by changing the way Australians eat. We make it easy for people to eat well, whether they want to lose weight or simply stay healthy. As a quality driven food company, all of our creative energies go into making our meals as delicious as they are healthy.




Are you a Lean practitioner with a passion for weaving together human capability and cutting-edge technology? Lite n' Easy is looking for a specialist to spearhead our transformation journey in Brisbane.

This is newly created role is designed for a forward-thinker who can bridge the gap between today’s processes and tomorrow’s possibilities. You won’t just be managing projects; you will be the designer of our automation roadmap, partnering with senior leadership to foster a high-performance culture that thrives on innovation and steady progress.

The Role:

Reporting to the Operations Manager (QLD/NSW), you will be the driving force behind site-wide evolution:

Automation Strategy: Lead the vision for our digital future. You will oversee the integration of robotics and vision systems, guiding the journey from initial concept to the seamless commissioning of advanced technologies

Lean Leadership: Act as the site’s CI mentor. You’ll establish Lean frameworks (5S, Kanban, SMED) and facilitate Kaizen events, empowering our frontline teams to take ownership of their own efficiency and growth.

Operational Impact: Take ownership of site-wide health. Your success is measured by the tangible smoothing of workflows, the elimination of waste, and the sustained improvement of our manufacturing and supply chain agility.

Digital Insights: Translate complex operational data into clear, actionable stories. Using Power BI and advanced analytics, you will provide the roadmap that helps the business understand the long-term value of our automation investments.

About you

The Experience: A deep background in automated supply chain environments, specifically with a "boots on the ground" understanding of commissioning sophisticated picking and sorting systems.

The Methodology: A Six Sigma mindset (Green Belt or higher) with a proven ability to lead cross-functional teams through the nuances of a Lean rollout.

The Technical Edge: A natural comfort with Power BI and a working curiosity for the interplay between PLC/SCADA systems and vision technology.

The Influence: A communicator who builds bridges someone just as effective coaching on the shopfloor as they are articulating a strategic vision to the executive team.

The Foundation: A holistic understanding of how operational efficiency intersects with WHS and HACCP standards.

Why Lite n' Easy?

Shape the Future: Have a direct hand in defining what our digital and automation strategy looks like from the ground up.

Hybrid Flexibility: Enjoy a modern, Brisbane-based working model that respects your life outside of work.

High Visibility: Gain direct exposure to the Senior Leadership Team in a role where your autonomy and ideas carry significant weight.

If you're excited to take on your next challenge, lead from the front and contribute to one of Australia’s leading food companies, Apply now! Join us in shaping the future of healthy eating in Australia.  Candidates must have full work rights in Australia – Australian Citizen or New Zealand Citizen.

Experienced Chief Financial Officer required

Finexia Financial Group Limited (ASX:FNX) is an independent financial services firm, specialising in private credit, funds management and advisory. Finexia was listed on the ASX in 2015 and has offices in Sydney and Surfers Paradise.

As a result of structural changes within the Group, we now seek to add Chief Financial Officer to our Sydney office located in the CBD.




What you will do

In this dynamic position, your expertise will be pivotal in enhancing the strategy and operations for our financial services group.




Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership: Partner with the CEO and Board to define and execute the Group’s financial strategy, capital management, short and long-term growth objectives.

Financial Reporting & Planning: Oversee the preparation and presentation of comprehensive financial reports, budgets, and rolling forecasts for all entities.

Strategic Analysis: Lead business modelling, risk assessment, financial due diligence and strategic initiatives.

ASX Compliance: Manage all ASX listing rule requirements, including half-year and annual reporting, continuous disclosure obligations, and regulatory filings.

Tax: Manage tax compliance and planning.

Audit: Coordinate external audits and planning.

Operational Finance: Enhance and automate financial systems and processes to drive efficiency. Oversee AP, AR & payroll functions.

Commercial Advisory: Provide high-level commercial insights on legal agreements, complex contracts, and strategic partnerships.

Capital Allocation: Drive cost-efficiency and performance metrics across the Group’s portfolio.

Investor Relations: Lead the Group’s investor relations strategy, managing communications with shareholders, conducting analyst briefings, and representing the company at any investor events.




Requirements

Qualifications: Bachelor's Degree in Finance or Accounting. CA/CPA is required.

Exceptional knowledge on AASB and IAS accounting standards.

Highly skilled tax effect understanding for consolidated group entities.

Experience: 5+ years of progressive finance experience, with a significant portion in senior leadership (CFO, VP Finance, or Head of Finance) within an ASX-listed environment.

Public Markets: Proven track record in public market reporting and corporate governance.

Technical Excellence: Exceptional financial modelling capabilities and the ability to translate complex data into actionable strategic insights.

Communication: A sophisticated communicator able to engage effectively with the Board, institutional investors, and stakeholders.

Legal Acumen: Proven ability to navigate, review, and interpret complex legal and regulatory contracts.

Mindset: An energetic, proactive, and entrepreneurial attitude with meticulous attention to detail and a "hands-on" approach to problem-solving.




Finexia is no ordinary firm. Things move fast here and we respond swiftly to new opportunities and challenges. Whilst we are somewhat flexible, this role is generally fulltime in the office.

Applicants are invited to submit a resume of no more than 4 pages. Please include a brief cover letter addressed to the CEO. This letter should showcase how you can contribute towards the success of Finexia. Applications without this cover letter will not be considered.

Applications close on 30 March 2026. Only candidates shortlisted for this role will be contacted.

Applicant introductions via recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

Senior Business Analyst - Payments - FTC

Location: Melbourne (remote)
Contract length: 12 months (definite extensions)
Salary: $130-160k + super

Role Overview

As a Business Analyst, you will play a key role in bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions. You will focus heavily on payments-related initiatives - including card systems, compliance, fraud, and system integrations, while also supporting broader digital transformation projects across core banking, CRM and digital channels.

Key Responsibilities

Lead and support payments-related initiatives with a focus on compliance, card security, Visa/Mastercard schemes, and fraud prevention.
Analyse and document business requirements for integrations across systems such as core banking, CRM, SMS gateways, data warehouses, and digital banking platforms.
Work closely with developers and architects to deliver API integrations and support microservices architecture (e.g., MuleSoft, middleware).
Ensure solutions align with the Banking Code of Practice, regulatory standards, and internal risk policies.
Translate complex technical concepts into clear business requirements and user stories.
Support end-to-end project delivery including business case development, stakeholder engagement, UAT planning, and change management.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including Product, Risk, Operations and Technology.

Required Skills & Experience

5+ years of experience as a Technical Business Analyst in the banking or financial services industry.
Demonstrated expertise in payments systems (NPP, BPAY, international payments), with a preference for deep knowledge in card payments, card security, and fraud detection.
Strong understanding of Visa/Mastercard ecosystems and compliance frameworks.
Proven experience working on API integrations, microservices, and middleware platforms (e.g., MuleSoft).
Familiarity with core banking systems, digital banking platforms, CRM tools, and data warehouses.
Strong knowledge of payments compliance and regulatory requirements in the Australian banking environment.
Experience with Banking Code of Practice and customer fairness principles.
Ability to work in agile environments and manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, evolving setting.
Excellent communication, documentation, and stakeholder management skills.

If the above seems like a good fit, please apply and we will be in touch if your profile is suitable!
